title: CARB Opens $7.3 Million in Community Planning and Capacity Building Grants for Clean Transportation
content:
The California Air Resources Board (CARB) has opened applications for its 2026 Community Planning and Capacity Building Grants, making up to $7.3 million available to support community-led clean transportation planning efforts across the state. The competitive grant program will provide individual awards of up to $500,000 to eligible applicants, including community-based organizations, local governments, public schools, and tribal governments.

The grants are intended to support early-stage planning and capacity-building activities that prepare communities for future clean mobility investments. Funded activities may include community transportation needs assessments, resident and stakeholder engagement, partnership development, workforce training and development, outreach, technical assistance, and other planning efforts related to clean and equitable transportation solutions. The program emphasizes community-driven approaches and local leadership in shaping transportation strategies.

A key update in the 2026 funding round is the removal of the Limited Waiver of Sovereign Immunity requirement for tribal governments. This change reduces administrative barriers and expands access to the program for tribal applicants seeking to participate in clean transportation planning initiatives.

The grants are funded through California Climate Investments, a statewide program that uses proceeds from Californiaâ€™s Cap-and-Invest program to support projects that reduce greenhouse gas emissions, strengthen the economy, and deliver public health and environmental benefits, particularly in disadvantaged communities.

CARB will host two rounds of informational Q&A sessions for prospective applicants in January, including sessions specifically for tribal applicants. Free technical assistance is available through CARBâ€™s Planning and Capacity Building Administrator team, with applicants required to complete a technical assistance survey by January 23, 2026 to guarantee support. Applications for the grant program are due by February 10, 2026.
author: Analysts Account
